programme.title: The United Way

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The next stage of lemur introductions keeps the primate care staff on their toes, while gibbons Kim and Tien head back to their familiar stomping ground. Tuans orangutans keep it cool with giant ice lollies.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: In Dao Tien a group of misbehaving doucs need to be relocated and female golden-cheeked gibbon Tungs release is hampered by the weather. While rain doesn't stop gibbons Kim and Tien going out into their tree filled enclosure.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Following the lives of orphas in the Nyaru Menteng Orangutan Rescue Centre in Borneo, the largest rehabilitation centre of its type in the world. programme.title: Valley of Flood and Fire
programme.desc: Documentary following the Luangwa Valley's transition from lush paradise to parched desert. It's the end of November and the rains have begun, ending a brutal dry season and beginning the transition to luscious wetland.

programme.desc: A retired couple seeks a beachfront condo to rent out and to host their big family with lots of grandchildren. They entrust a contractor to make extensive cosmetic updates to an outdated unit to turn it into their dream beach getaway.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A young couple renovate a vacation property in Crystal Beach, Texas, that is just a ferry ride away from their home.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple is ready to find a beach home of their own after years of visiting relatives on the sunny shores of Galveston, Texas, but the house they find is need of serious work.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: In Middlebourne, W.Va., the "Barnwood Builders" save an exceptional log cabin and help a local sheriff return to his family farm; in Alabama, Mark Bowe visits the finished lakeside cabin designed by Karen Tillery.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The crew takes the week off to work in their own backyards; in West Virginia, Kentucky, Pennsylvania and Virginia, the guys build furniture, cut trees, and plant gardens; Mark Bowe visits the finished Catskill cabin in Shawangunk, N.Y.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys wade in water to save a dilapidated springhouse, then they rebuild it back at the bone yard.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys take the 100 percent cabin to South Carolina where they rebuild it as a hunting cabin.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys explore a pioneer settlement, where they save a classic log cabin and re-purpose its rare hand-cut sandstone chimney; Sherman helps local stonemasons turn the chimney into a fire pit.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys have to build their own road to reach a cabin that has been overgrown for 11 years; while Rose never got to finish her dream cabin, Mark salvages some of its wood to make a rose inlaid table for her daughter.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the crew are tasked with the relocation and land manifestation of a yurt on an island on Togus Pond; the team seeks to beat the ice and snow so they can have the yurt ready for the family to drop their fishing lines in the frozen pond.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the team seek to transform a family's 1950s lobster shack into a cabin that will create a lifetime of memories for generations to come; they re-imagine the cabin's interior layout, preserve a mural and add an exterior widow's walk.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple's Belle Island camp has a long, multi-generational history within their family spanning nearly 100 years, and they call on the Cabin Masters to bring it into the present day while preserving it for the next generation.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple inherit a quaint cabin; Chase and his team focus on transforming the former fish and lobster shack into a fun family cabin.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: In Oakland, Maine, Chase and his team take on a project on McGrath Pond that has been empty for 15 years; while it seems like a pretty straightforward renovation, starting a project this late in the season raises a lot of challenges.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family has created lasting memories of their cabin overlooking Harpswell Sound, but the cabin is in need of some work to see the next 20 years and beyond; Chase and the team expand it to accommodate the next generation.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple calls on Chase and his team to help save their mountain camp which was leveled by a tornado; with an emphasis on outdoor entertaining, the team adds an open second floor while racing to wrap it up before the first snow falls.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team renovate a camp situated on two ponds; with winter right around the corner, Chase knows they need to hustle in order to complete the renovation in time for the homeowner's Thanksgiving get-together.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Agassiz Village provides underprivileged and at-risk kids an opportunity to experience a camp surrounded by the beauty of Maine; with support from the community, Chase and his team take on the big project with a small budget to help the camp.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the gang team up with sports broadcaster Dan Patrick whose family compound is in need of a new bunkhouse; the project starts from scratch, so the Maine Cabin Masters need their whole roster to pitch in.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The "Maine Cabin Masters" are here to help out a family with one of their unique builds yet, a geodesic dome.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: This family's camp is cozy and complete on the inside, but the outside is a mess.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The team helps Matthew Dix restore his grandfather's old shed to its former glory; with Dixie's grandmother waiting impatiently next door, the crew members visit every chance they get to put hammer to nails to restore the decrepit cabin.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his team take on a dated and dysfunctional cabin in Belgrade Lakes; with a budget of $40,000 and six weeks to finish the project, the team strives to turn this Long Pond cabin into the perfect family retreat.
